Main Entrance |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto}} |Sacred_Object = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Myōjin Taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Disputed Shrines|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main_Building = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Other_names = Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine, Minouchi Taisha, Hikokamiwake Shrine |Festivals = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|WD=Q2853|lt=September 10}} |Rituals = }} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社, ''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Jinja'') is a shrine located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, specifically a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, although its status remains disputed among scholars. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a prefectural shrine. The shrine is also known colloquially as "Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine," "Minouchi Taisha," and "Hikokamiwake Shrine." == Overview == [[File:Yokoyamajyo.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Torii
Inscription "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine" by Prince Arisugawa Taruhito]] The shrine is located in {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Jōyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426101}}, within the ruins of {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Yokoyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542535}} and adjacent to the east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji}}. It was originally situated north of Zenkoji’s main hall as "Toshigami-dō" (Year God Hall), serving as a protective shrine for Zenkoji. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} movement at the beginning of the Meiji period, it was relocated to its current location. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto}}, the same deity worshipped at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}}. On its grounds, {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira}} are erected, and the shrine participates in the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival}} in the years of the Tiger and Monkey, rotating with {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto}}''' (たけみなかたとみのみこと) :: The enshrined deity is identical to that of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}}. == History == The shrine's establishment date is unknown. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine of Minouchi District, Shinano Province." However, there is still debate about its exact location, with other possible sites in {{ill|Shinshu Shinmachi|ja|信州新町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu Shinmachi|1=Shinshūshinmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042382}} and {{ill|Iiyama, Nagano|en|Iiyama, Nagano|WD=Q851097|lt=Iiyama City}} also claiming the title. Until 1878 (Meiji 11), the shrine was located within Zenkoji’s grounds and known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." A record from 1356 in the "Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba" describes Toshigami-dō as a branch shrine of Suwa Taisha, claiming it to be the "Minouchi Shrine" mentioned in the 5th year of Empress Jitō’s reign. In January 1879 (Meiji 12), during Emperor Meiji’s visit, the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} movement. It was renamed to match the name recorded in the Engishiki. Only the enshrined deity was moved; the original building was transferred to {{ill|Morita no Shrine|ja|守田迺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita no Shrine|1=Moritano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450096}}. == Grounds == File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall The shrine grounds feature various stone monuments. == Sub-Shrines == * Mokushōso Shrine - Enshrined in 1881 (Meiji 14). Rebuilt in 1974 (Showa 49). * Chihata Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内社.JPG|Mokushōso Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内千幡社.JPG|Chihata Shrine == location == ; Address * {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} ; Transportation * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway): ''' Take the Nagaden Bus (Routes 1–4) to "Shiroyama Park Entrance" bus stop. ''' Take the Alpico Kotsu (Kawanakajima Bus) Routes 11, 16, or 17 to "Zenkoji North" bus stop. ; Nearby * {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11262830}} ruins (located on the former main bailey) * {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426097}} ''' {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Art Museum|en|Nagano Zoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654175}} ''' {{ill|Nagano City Youth Science Center|ja|長野市少年科学センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Youth Science Center|1=Nagano City Youth Science Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654177}} ''' {{ill|Shinano Shokonsha Shrine|ja|信濃招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shokonsha Shrine|1=Shinano-shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386178}} * {{ill|Nagano Local Meteorological Observatory|ja|長野地方気象台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Local Meteorological Main Entrance |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |Sacred_Object = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Myōjin Taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Disputed Shrines|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main_Building = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Other_names = Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine, Minouchi Taisha, Hikokamiwake Shrine |Festivals = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|WD=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} |Rituals = }} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社, ''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Jinja'') is a shrine located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, specifically a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, although its status remains disputed among scholars. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a prefectural shrine. The shrine is also known colloquially as "Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine," "Minouchi Taisha," and "Hikokamiwake Shrine." == Overview == [[File:Yokoyamajyo.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Torii
Inscription "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine" by Prince Arisugawa Taruhito]] The shrine is located in {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Jōyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426101}}, within the ruins of {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Yokoyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542535}} and adjacent to the east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji|1=Zenkō-ji}}. It was originally situated north of Zenkoji’s main hall as "Toshigami-dō" (Year God Hall), serving as a protective shrine for Zenkoji. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement at the beginning of the Meiji period, it was relocated to its current location. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, the same deity worshipped at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. On its grounds, {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira|1=Onbashira}} are erected, and the shrine participates in the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} in the years of the Tiger and Monkey, rotating with {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}''' (たけみなかたとみのみこと) :: The enshrined deity is identical to that of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The shrine's establishment date is unknown. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine of Minouchi District, Shinano Province." However, there is still debate about its exact location, with other possible sites in {{ill|Shinshu Shinmachi|ja|信州新町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu Shinmachi|1=Shinshūshinmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042382}} and {{ill|Iiyama, Nagano|en|Iiyama, Nagano|WD=Q851097|lt=Iiyama City|1=Iiyama, Nagano}} also claiming the title. Until 1878 (Meiji 11), the shrine was located within Zenkoji’s grounds and known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." A record from 1356 in the "Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba" describes Toshigami-dō as a branch shrine of Suwa Taisha, claiming it to be the "Minouchi Shrine" mentioned in the 5th year of Empress Jitō’s reign. In January 1879 (Meiji 12), during Emperor Meiji’s visit, the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement. It was renamed to match the name recorded in the Engishiki. Only the enshrined deity was moved; the original building was transferred to {{ill|Morita no Shrine|ja|守田迺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita no Shrine|1=Moritano Shrine (Minochi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450096}}. == Grounds == File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall The shrine grounds feature various stone monuments. == Sub-Shrines == * Mokushōso Shrine - Enshrined in 1881 (Meiji 14). [[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)]] [[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)]] [[ja:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 正面.JPG|280px]]
Main Entrance |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|39|41.54|N|138|11|31.71|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |Sacred_Object = |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|lt=Myōjin Taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|Disputed Shrines|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main_Building = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Other_names = Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine, Minouchi Taisha, Hikokamiwake Shrine |Festivals = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|WD=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} |Rituals = }} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社, ''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Jinja'') is a shrine located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, specifically a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, although its status remains disputed among scholars. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a prefectural shrine. The shrine is also known colloquially as "Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine," "Minouchi Taisha," and "Hikokamiwake Shrine." == Overview == [[File:Yokoyamajyo.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Torii
Inscription "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine" by Prince Arisugawa Taruhito]] The shrine is located in {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Jōyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426101}}, within the ruins of {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Yokoyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542535}} and adjacent to the east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji|1=Zenkō-ji}}. It was originally situated north of Zenkoji’s main hall as "Toshigami-dō" (Year God Hall), serving as a protective shrine for Zenkoji. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement at the beginning of the Meiji period, it was relocated to its current location. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, the same deity worshipped at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. On its grounds, {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira|1=Onbashira}} are erected, and the shrine participates in the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} in the years of the Tiger and Monkey, rotating with {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}''' (たけみなかたとみのみこと) :: The enshrined deity is identical to that of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The shrine's establishment date is unknown. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine of Minouchi District, Shinano Province." However, there is still debate about its exact location, with other possible sites in {{ill|Shinshu Shinmachi|ja|信州新町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu Shinmachi|1=Shinshūshinmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042382}} and {{ill|Iiyama, Nagano|en|Iiyama, Nagano|WD=Q851097|lt=Iiyama City|1=Iiyama, Nagano}} also claiming the title. Until 1878 (Meiji 11), the shrine was located within Zenkoji’s grounds and known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." A record from 1356 in the "Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba" describes Toshigami-dō as a branch shrine of Suwa Taisha, claiming it to be the "Minouchi Shrine" mentioned in the 5th year of Empress Jitō’s reign. In January 1879 (Meiji 12), during Emperor Meiji’s visit, the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement. It was renamed to match the name recorded in the Engishiki. Only the enshrined deity was moved; the original building was transferred to {{ill|Morita no Shrine|ja|守田迺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita no Shrine|1=Moritano Shrine (Minochi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450096}}. == Grounds == File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall The shrine grounds feature various stone monuments. == Sub-Shrines == * Mokushōso Shrine - Enshrined in 1881 (Meiji 14). Rebuilt in 1974 (Showa 49). * Chihata Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内社.JPG|Mokushōso Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内千幡社.JPG|Chihata Shrine == location == ; Address * {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ; Transportation * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway): ''' Take the Nagaden Bus (Routes 1–4) to "Shiroyama Park Entrance" bus stop. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main_Building = Single-bay nagare-zukuri |Other_names = Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine, Minouchi Taisha, Hikokamiwake Shrine |Festivals = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|WD=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} |Rituals = }} '''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine''' (健御名方富命彦神別神社, ''Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Jinja'') is a shrine located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, specifically a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, although its status remains disputed among scholars. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a prefectural shrine. The shrine is also known colloquially as "Shiroyama Prefectural Shrine," "Minouchi Taisha," and "Hikokamiwake Shrine." == Overview == [[File:Yokoyamajyo.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Torii
Inscription "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine" by Prince Arisugawa Taruhito]] The shrine is located in {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Jōyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426101}}, within the ruins of {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Yokoyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542535}} and adjacent to the east of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji|1=Zenkō-ji}}. It was originally situated north of Zenkoji’s main hall as "Toshigami-dō" (Year God Hall), serving as a protective shrine for Zenkoji. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement at the beginning of the Meiji period, it was relocated to its current location. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, the same deity worshipped at {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. On its grounds, {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira|1=Onbashira}} are erected, and the shrine participates in the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} in the years of the Tiger and Monkey, rotating with {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}}. == deity == * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}''' (たけみなかたとみのみこと) :: The enshrined deity is identical to that of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The shrine's establishment date is unknown. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the name "Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine of Minouchi District, Shinano Province." However, there is still debate about its exact location, with other possible sites in {{ill|Shinshu Shinmachi|ja|信州新町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshu Shinmachi|1=Shinshūshinmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042382}} and {{ill|Iiyama, Nagano|en|Iiyama, Nagano|WD=Q851097|lt=Iiyama City|1=Iiyama, Nagano}} also claiming the title. Until 1878 (Meiji 11), the shrine was located within Zenkoji’s grounds and known as "Toshigami-dō Hachiman Shrine." A record from 1356 in the "Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba" describes Toshigami-dō as a branch shrine of Suwa Taisha, claiming it to be the "Minouchi Shrine" mentioned in the 5th year of Empress Jitō’s reign. In January 1879 (Meiji 12), during Emperor Meiji’s visit, the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement. It was renamed to match the name recorded in the Engishiki. Only the enshrined deity was moved; the original building was transferred to {{ill|Morita no Shrine|ja|守田迺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita no Shrine|1=Moritano Shrine (Minochi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450096}}. == Grounds == File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall The shrine grounds feature various stone monuments. == Sub-Shrines == * Mokushōso Shrine - Enshrined in 1881 (Meiji 14). Rebuilt in 1974 (Showa 49). * Chihata Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内社.JPG|Mokushōso Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内千幡社.JPG|Chihata Shrine == location == ; Address * {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ; Transportation * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway): ''' Take the Nagaden Bus (Routes 1–4) to "Shiroyama Park Entrance" bus stop. ''' Take the Alpico Kotsu (Kawanakajima Bus) Routes 11, 16, or 17 to "Zenkoji North" bus stop. ; Nearby * {{ill|Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|ja|横山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Castle (Shinano Province)|1=Q11262830|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11262830}} ruins (located on the former main bailey) * {{ill|Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|ja|城山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Park (Nagano)|1=Q11426097|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426097}} ''' {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Art Museum|en|Nagano Prefectural Zoo|1=Nagano City Jōyama Zoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654175}} ''' {{ill|Nagano City Youth Science Center|ja|長野市少年科学センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano City Youth Science Center|1=Q11654177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654177}} ''' {{ill|Shinano Shokonsha Shrine|ja|信濃招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shokonsha Shrine|1=Shinano-shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386178}} * {{ill|Nagano Local Meteorological Observatory|ja|長野地方気象台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Local Meteorological {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji|1=Zenkō-ji}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * *Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia Nagano Prefecture Vol.* (Heibonsha) The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, specifically a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, although its status remains disputed among scholars. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the Rank of a prefectural shrine. In January 1879 (Meiji 12), during Emperor Meiji’s visit, the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} movement. It was renamed to match the name recorded in the Engishiki. Only the enshrined deity was moved; the original building was transferred to {{ill|Morita no Shrine|ja|守田迺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita no Shrine|1=Moritano Shrine (Minochi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450096}}. == Grounds == File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall The shrine grounds feature various stone monuments. == Sub-Shrines == * Mokushōso Shrine - Enshrined in 1881 (Meiji 14). Rebuilt in 1974 (Showa 49). * Chihata Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内社.JPG|Mokushōso Shrine File:健御名方富命彦神別神社 (長野市箱清水) 境内千幡社.JPG|Chihata Shrine == location == ; Address * {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}} Ōaza Nagano Aza Honjo Higashi 2411, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} ; Transportation * From {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East and Nagano Electric Railway): ''' Take the Nagaden Bus (Routes 1–4) to "Shiroyama Park Entrance" bus stop.
